261	# =================================================================================================
262	# THE REGISTRY — the ONE table that says what each artifact kind IS and when it is COMPLETE.
263	# =================================================================================================
264	# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
265	#
266	# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
267	# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
268	# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
269	# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
270	# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
271	#
272	# `expression` is the expression kind these bytes CONSTITUTE when they are the document — the link
273	# between "what is this artifact" and "what may a span in it name". A judicial opinion's bytes are its
274	# `official_text`; there is no journal version of it to widen to, ever.
275	#
276	# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
277	# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
278	# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
279	# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
280	# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
281	# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
282	
283	SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200     # scholarly kinds ONLY. It is not a fact about documents in general.
284	SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR = 120  # ...and below THIS, a scholarly fragment is a citation stub.
285	
286	KIND_PROFILE: dict[str, dict] = {
287	    'journal_article':       dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
288	                                  expression='journal_version',     finding_bearing=True),
289	    'proceedings_paper':     dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
290	                                  expression='proceedings_version', finding_bearing=True),
291	    'working_paper':         dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
292	                                  expression='working_paper',       finding_bearing=True),
293	    'preprint':              dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
294	                                  expression='preprint',            finding_bearing=True),
295	    'accepted_manuscript':   dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
296	                                  expression='accepted_manuscript', finding_bearing=True),
297	    # A short official opinion IS the whole artifact. No results section, no methods, and COMPLETE.
298	    # stub_floor=None IS THE POINT OF THIS TABLE. Do not put a number here. Ever.
299	    'judicial_opinion':      dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
300	                                  expression='official_text',   finding_bearing=True),
301	    'statute_section':       dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
302	                                  expression='official_text',   finding_bearing=True),
303	    'trial_registry_record': dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
304	                                  expression='registry_record', finding_bearing=True),
305	    # These are NEVER complete, at any length, and may never carry a finding. They have no expression:
306	    # a cookie banner is not a VERSION of the paper, it is not the paper at all.
307	    'abstract':              dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
308	    'citation_only':         dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
309	    'landing_page':          dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
310	    'extraction_failure':    dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
311	    'wrong_work':            dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
312	    'unknown':               dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
313	}
314	# DERIVED from the registry, never maintained beside it — two lists of kinds will drift, and the one
315	# that drifts is always the one a gate reads.
316	ARTIFACT_KINDS = tuple(KIND_PROFILE)
317	FINDING_BEARING_KINDS = tuple(k for k, v in KIND_PROFILE.items() if v['finding_bearing'])
318	
319	#: NOT A DOCUMENT AT ALL — as distinct from `unknown`, which IS a document whose VERSION we cannot
320	#: establish. Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) is 10,902 readable words whose title page is (cid:N) font
321	#: codes: we HOLD a document and cannot say which version it is. A cookie banner is not a document.
322	#: Reporting those two in one bucket would say "we hold nothing" about a paper we hold in full, which
323	#: is a different claim, and a false one.
324	NOT_A_DOCUMENT_KINDS = tuple(k for k, v in KIND_PROFILE.items()
325	                             if not v['finding_bearing'] and k != 'unknown')
326	
327	#: WHICH COMPLETENESS PROFILE DOES THIS WORK'S DOCUMENT GET? `None` => the scholarly family, whose
328	#: artifact kind is derived from the bytes' own version furniture. Everything else is fixed by the
329	#: work: a case produces judicial opinions, and no amount of NBER letterhead makes one a working paper.
330	WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT: dict[str, str | None] = {
331	    'study':   None,
332	    'case':    'judicial_opinion',
333	    'statute': 'statute_section',
334	    'trial':   'trial_registry_record',
335	}
336	WORK_KINDS = tuple(WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT)
337	
338	#: THE ROW'S OWN DECLARED TYPE -> (work kind, the expression the METADATA claims exists).
339	#: `migrate()` used to hardcode a `journal_version` for every row in the corpus. Sol: "That is not
340	#: valid for a judicial opinion or statute." The row DECLARES its type — every one of the 70 rows here
341	#: carries `type: 'journal-article'` from Crossref, and the code never once read it. It assumed the
342	#: answer it happened to be right about, which is not the same as being right.
343	SOURCE_TYPE: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
344	    'journal-article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
345	    'journal article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
346	    'proceedings-article': ('study',   'proceedings_version'),
347	    'book-chapter':        ('study',   'journal_version'),
348	    'posted-content':      ('study',   'preprint'),      # a preprint row claims NO journal version
349	    'preprint':            ('study',   'preprint'),
350	    'report':              ('study',   'working_paper'),
351	    'working-paper':       ('study',   'working_paper'),
352	    'judicial-opinion':    ('case',    'official_text'),
353	    'opinion':             ('case',    'official_text'),
354	    'case':                ('case',    'official_text'),
355	    'statute':             ('statute', 'official_text'),
356	    'legislation':         ('statute', 'official_text'),
357	    'clinical-trial':      ('trial',   'registry_record'),
358	    'trial-registration':  ('trial',   'registry_record'),
359	}
360	
361	RESULT_BEARING = ('results', 'tables', 'analysis')
362	
363	
364	def judge_completeness(artifact_kind: str, n_words: int,
365	                       extraction_verdict: str = 'CLEAN') -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
366	    """THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. `profile()`, `Graph.from_json()` and
367	    `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` all come through here and through nothing else.
368	
369	    THERE IS NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD IN THIS FUNCTION AND THERE MUST NEVER BE ONE. Completeness is

600	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
601	    )
602	
603	
604	# =================================================================================================
605	# 3. THE GRAPH
606	# =================================================================================================
607	
608	class SpanBindingError(ValueError):
609	    """A span that cannot be bound to real bytes. Not a warning: THE BINDING DOES NOT EXIST."""
610	
611	
612	class GraphIntegrityError(ValueError):
613	    """A graph on disk that does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never repaired."""
614	
615	
616	# What it takes to ASSERT that a span survives from one expression into another. Only a claim about
617	# BYTES can do it -- that is the entire content of the word "span-preserving".
618	_AUTHENTICATES = re.compile(
619	    r'byte[- ]for[- ]byte|byte[- ]level|checksum|sha-?256|content[_ -]hash'
620	    r'|identical (?:bytes|text|spans?)|span[- ]level (?:diff|comparison)'
621	    r'|(?:diffed|compared) (?:it )?against the .{0,30}(?:pdf|bytes|text|version)', re.I)
622	_PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES = re.compile(
623	    r'\btitles?\b|\bmetadata\b|\bsimilar\b|\bplausib|\blooks? like\b|self[- ]describ|\bassum', re.I)
624	
625	
626	def authentication_failure(basis: str) -> str:
627	    """'' if `basis` authenticates SPAN PRESERVATION; otherwise the reason it does not.
628	
629	    Sol, verbatim: "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
630	    exact_copy_of."
631	
632	    An ASSERTED span-preserving edge is the ONLY thing in this graph that lets a span name a document
633	    WHOSE BYTES WE DO NOT HOLD. It is therefore the only thing worth attacking, and it was guarded on
634	    ONE of its two types: the old check read `type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis`. But
635	    `accepted_manuscript_of` is span-preserving too, and it widens attribution identically — so
636	
637	        add_edge(wp, journal, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'ASSERTED', 'the titles match')
638	
639	    was ACCEPTED, and it made the working paper's spans journal-attributable. The rule is not "no
640	    title matches for exact_copy_of". The rule is: an ASSERTED span-preserving edge must name
641	    BYTE-LEVEL EVIDENCE, whatever it is called. Both directions are enforced here, in ONE predicate,
642	    used by `add_edge()` at construction AND by `from_json()` at load — because an edge that cannot be
643	    constructed but CAN BE LOADED is not a rule, it is a speed bump.
644	    """
645	    if not basis or not basis.strip():
646	        return 'an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it'
647	    weak = _PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES.search(basis)
648	    if weak:
649	        return (f'the basis rests on {weak.group(0)!r}, which proves NOTHING ABOUT BYTES — PEER REVIEW '
650	                f'CHANGES NUMBERS, and matching titles/metadata cannot show a span survived it')
651	    if not _AUTHENTICATES.search(basis):
652	        return ('the basis names no BYTE-LEVEL evidence (a byte-for-byte / checksum / span-level '
653	                'comparison against the target expression). Nothing else can ASSERT span preservation')
654	    return ''
655	
656	
657	@dataclass(frozen=True)
658	class SourcePolicy:
659	    """WHICH EXPRESSIONS THIS ANSWER IS ALLOWED TO CITE. The task's instruction, made mechanical.
660	
661	    This is an ARGUMENT, not a stored field, because admissibility is a property of THE ANSWER and not
662	    of the bytes. The same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under "journal articles only" and
663	    admissible under "any identified version"; a boolean on the node has to be wrong for one of them.
664	    `Expression.citable_in_journal_only_answer` was exactly that boolean, and it is gone.
665	    """
666	    name: str
667	    permitted_expression_kinds: tuple[str, ...]
668	    require_complete: bool = True
669	
670	
671	#: Task 72. "If only the working paper is available, citing it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION."
672	JOURNAL_ONLY  = SourcePolicy('journal_articles_only', ('journal_version',))
673	PEER_REVIEWED = SourcePolicy('peer_reviewed', ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'))
674	#: For a legal or clinical question, the authoritative text IS the source. There is no journal here.
675	OFFICIAL_TEXT = SourcePolicy('official_text_only', ('official_text', 'registry_record'))
676	ANY_VERSION   = SourcePolicy('any_identified_version',
677	                             ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript',
678	                              'working_paper', 'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record'))
679	
680	
681	@dataclass(frozen=True)
682	class Attribution:
683	    """The resolved answer to: may a span from these bytes be attributed AT ALL, and TO WHAT?"""
684	    manifestation_id: str
685	    content_hash: str
686	    expression_id: str                        # the expression whose bytes these ARE
687	    permitted_expression_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # everything a span here may name (IDs — never prose)
688	    policy: str
689	    admitted: bool
690	    names_expression_id: str | None           # THE id the sentence must name. None => name NOTHING.
691	    text: str | None                          # display cache. NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
692	    refusal: str | None
693	
694	
695	@dataclass
696	class Graph:
697	    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)
698	    expressions: dict[str, Expression] = field(default_factory=dict)
699	    manifestations: dict[str, Manifestation] = field(default_factory=dict)
700	    edges: list[Edge] = field(default_factory=list)
701	
702	    # -- construction -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
703	    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
704	        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
705	            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
706	        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
707	            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
708	        if not basis or not basis.strip():
709	            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
710	            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
711	        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
712	            # Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it, and re-enforced in
713	            # from_json() so no FILE can either.
714	            why = authentication_failure(basis)
715	            if why:
716	                raise ValueError(f'cannot ASSERT {type} — {why}')
717	        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
718	        self.edges.append(e)
719	        return e
720	
721	    # -- THE CORE RULE ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
722	    def attribution_targets(self, manifestation_id: str) -> list[str]:
723	        """Which expressions a span in this manifestation MAY name. Sol's rule, mechanised.
724	
725	        Always: the manifestation's own expression -- the bytes we hold are, definitionally, that
726	        expression's bytes. Additionally: any expression reachable by an ASSERTED, SPAN-PRESERVING
727	        edge, because such an edge is exactly a proof that the cited source contains the same span.
728	        Nothing else. A `predecessor_of` edge, however obvious, transfers NOTHING.
729	        """
730	        m = self.manifestations[manifestation_id]
731	        targets = [m.expression_id]
732	        for e in self.edges:
733	            if e.src == m.expression_id and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING and e.status == 'ASSERTED':
734	                if e.dst not in targets:
735	                    targets.append(e.dst)
736	        return targets
737	
738	    # -- THE API. THREE FUNCTIONS. --------------------------------------------------------------
739	    def bind_span(self, manifestation_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> dict:
740	        """A span binds to its EXACT manifestation and content hash. This is what a card must carry
741	        instead of a bare DOI -- a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes.
742	
743	        BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED HERE. THEY WERE NOT, AND PYTHON IS SILENT ABOUT NONSENSE: for a
744	        1,000-char text, `text[-5:20]`, `text[900:100]` and `text[5000:6000]` are all `''`. Every one
745	        of those is a span that BINDS CLEANLY AND THEN VERIFIES — its stored text is empty, the empty
746	        slice still equals it, and the content hash is perfectly correct. The card would name a real
747	        paper, carry a real hash, pass verify_span(), and quote NOTHING FROM IT. That is a fabricated
748	        citation with a valid receipt, which is the exact shape of every defect in this file's header.
749	        A negative offset is worse than empty: `text[-5:]` silently indexes from the END and binds a
750	        span from somewhere else entirely.
751	
752	        So they are REFUSED, not clamped. Clamping would invent a span the miner never mined.
753	
754	        RETURNS IDENTIFIERS, NOT PROSE. `may_name` used to hand back English SENTENCES, so a consumer
755	        deciding what was legal had to string-match a display string. Prose is a cache; an id resolves.
756	        """
757	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
758	        if m is None:
759	            raise SpanBindingError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r} — a span cannot bind to '
760	                                   f'bytes we do not hold')
761	        for nm, v in (('start', start), ('end', end)):
762	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
763	                raise SpanBindingError(f'{nm} offset must be an int, got {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')
764	        n = len(m.text)
765	        if start < 0 or end < 0:
766	            raise SpanBindingError(
767	                f'NEGATIVE span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — Python would index from the '
768	                f'END of the text and bind a span from a different part of the document')
769	        if end <= start:
770	            raise SpanBindingError(
771	                f'EMPTY OR REVERSED span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — it slices to "" and '
772	                f'would then verify perfectly against nothing, under a real citation')
773	        if end > n:
774	            raise SpanBindingError(
775	                f'span [{start}:{end}] runs past the end of {manifestation_id} ({n} chars) — Python '
776	                f'would truncate it silently, and the stored text would not be the span that was mined')
777	        return dict(
778	            manifestation_id=manifestation_id,
779	            content_hash=m.content_hash,
780	            span_start=start, span_end=end,
781	            text=m.text[start:end],
782	            expression_id=m.expression_id,
783	            permitted_expression_ids=list(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id)),
784	        )
785	
786	    def resolve_attribution(self, manifestation_id: str,
787	                            source_policy: SourcePolicy = JOURNAL_ONLY) -> Attribution:
788	        """MAY a span from these bytes be attributed under THIS task's instruction, and TO WHAT ID?
789	
790	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
791	          1. the bytes are usable as evidence at all — complete, uncorrupted, the right work; AND
792	          2. some expression the span MAY LEGALLY NAME (own expression, or one across an ASSERTED
793	             span-preserving edge) is of a kind THIS POLICY PERMITS.
794	
795	        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss, whose
796	        verbatim span said "633 per cent" because the PDF was a Caesar-shifted encoding of "300".
797	        """
798	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
799	        if m is None:
800	            raise KeyError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r}')
801	        targets = tuple(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id))
802	        base = dict(manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
803	                    expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=targets,
804	                    policy=source_policy.name)
805	
806	        if source_policy.require_complete and not m.profile.get('complete'):
807	            why = '; '.join(m.profile.get('incomplete_because') or ['the bytes are not a usable document'])
808	            return Attribution(**base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
809	                               refusal=f'these bytes may not carry a finding: {why}')
810	
811	        # Prefer the expression whose bytes these ACTUALLY ARE. Widening is a fallback, never a first
812	        # choice: naming the document we hold is always the strongest true thing we can say.
813	        permitted = [t for t in targets
814	                     if self.expressions[t].kind in source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds]
815	        if not permitted:
816	            kinds = ', '.join(sorted({self.expressions[t].kind for t in targets})) or 'nothing'
817	            return Attribution(
818	                **base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
819	                refusal=(f'policy `{source_policy.name}` permits '
820	                         f'{{{", ".join(source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds)}}}, and a span here '
821	                         f'may only name {{{kinds}}}. No ASSERTED span-preserving edge widens it — '
822	                         f'citing a permitted source with THESE bytes would be fabrication'))
823	        chosen = m.expression_id if m.expression_id in permitted else permitted[0]
824	        return Attribution(**base, admitted=True, names_expression_id=chosen,
825	                           text=self.expressions[chosen].attribution, refusal=None)
826	
827	    def verify_span(self, binding: dict) -> bool:
828	        """The enforcement point. A binding arriving here is AN UNTRUSTED INPUT — by now it has been
829	        through a JSON file — so this re-checks EVERYTHING bind_span() checked, and then the bytes.
830	
831	        A gate that trusts the offsets it is handed is a gate that verifies `text[900:100] == ''`.
832	        """
833	        try:
834	            mid = binding['manifestation_id']
835	            s, e = binding['span_start'], binding['span_end']
836	            stored, chash = binding['text'], binding['content_hash']
837	        except (KeyError, TypeError):
838	            return False
839	        m = self.manifestations.get(mid)
840	        if m is None or m.content_hash != chash:
841	            return False
842	        for v in (s, e):
843	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
844	                return False
845	        if s < 0 or e <= s or e > len(m.text):
846	            return False
847	        # the node itself must not have been edited since it was loaded: the hash is over THE TEXT.
848	        if hashlib.sha256(m.text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest() != m.content_hash:
849	            return False
850	        if m.text[s:e] != stored:
851	            return False
852	        # ...and the permitted set is not a cache. If an edge changed, the STORED permission is stale,
853	        # and a stale permission is how a span keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
854	        if 'permitted_expression_ids' in binding:
855	            if list(binding['permitted_expression_ids']) != list(self.attribution_targets(mid)):
856	                return False
857	        return True
858	
859	    # -- derived conveniences (they go THROUGH the API above; they are not a second door) ----------
860	    def journal_attributable(self, manifestation_id: str) -> bool:
861	        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE? (task 72)"""
862	        return self.resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted
863	
864	    # -- serialisation ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
865	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
866	        return dict(
867	            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
868	            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
869	            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
870	            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
871	            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
872	        )
873	
874	    @classmethod
875	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
876	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
877	
878	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us
879	        already. `to_json()` writes the text, its hash, its word count and its derived profile, and any
880	        copy of that file — hand-edited, half-written, merged, or produced by an older version of this
881	        module — can disagree with itself in every one of those places. A loader that accepts what it
882	        is handed re-opens the hole FROM THE OTHER SIDE: the enforcement point would read a
883	        `complete: true` that no bytes support, and would enforce it.
884	
885	        REFUSES, NEVER REPAIRS:
886	          - a content_hash that is not sha256(text)                  [the span gate's only anchor]
887	          - an n_words that is not len(text.split())                 [the count that describes a
888	                                                                       document we no longer hold —
889	                                                                       the exact defect event_ledger
890	                                                                       catches in the live corpus]
891	          - a stored extractability verdict the text does not support
892	          - a `complete: true` (or false) THE REGISTRY DOES NOT DERIVE for that kind and that text
893	          - a manifestation naming an expression or a work that does not exist
894	          - a manifestation whose expression belongs to a DIFFERENT work
895	          - an expression naming a work that does not exist
896	          - an edge whose endpoints do not exist, or that carries no basis
897	          - an ASSERTED span-preserving edge whose basis does not AUTHENTICATE span preservation
898	          - any unknown field, anywhere (a schema drift silently drops the field a gate reads)
899	        """
900	        if isinstance(data, str):
901	            data = json.loads(data)
902	        g = cls()
903	        err: list[str] = []
904	
905	        def _fields(cl) -> set[str]:
906	            return set(cl.__dataclass_fields__)
907	
908	        def _take(cl, d: dict, what: str):
909	            extra = set(d) - _fields(cl)
910	            if extra:
911	                err.append(f'{what}: unknown field(s) {sorted(extra)} — this graph was not written by '
912	                           f'this schema, and a field a gate reads may be missing from it')
913	                return None
914	            missing = _fields(cl) - set(d) - {'profile', 'kind'}
915	            if missing:
916	                err.append(f'{what}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
917	                return None
918	            return cl(**d)
919	
920	        for d in data.get('works', []):
921	            w = _take(Work, d, f"work {d.get('id')!r}")
922	            if w is None:
923	                continue
924	            if w.kind not in WORK_KINDS:
925	                err.append(f'work {w.id!r}: kind {w.kind!r} is not a registered work kind {WORK_KINDS}')
926	            g.works[w.id] = w
927	
928	        for d in data.get('expressions', []):
929	            e = _take(Expression, d, f"expression {d.get('id')!r}")
930	            if e is None:
931	                continue
932	            if e.kind not in EXPRESSION_KINDS:
933	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: kind {e.kind!r} is not in EXPRESSION_KINDS')
934	            if e.work_id not in g.works:
935	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: names work {e.work_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
936	            g.expressions[e.id] = e
937	
938	        for d in data.get('manifestations', []):
939	            m = _take(Manifestation, d, f"manifestation {d.get('id')!r}")
940	            if m is None:
941	                continue
942	            g.manifestations[m.id] = m
943	            where = f'manifestation {m.id!r}'
944	
945	            # 1. THE HASH IS THE ONLY ANCHOR THE SPAN GATE HAS.
946	            real = hashlib.sha256((m.text or '').encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
947	            if real != m.content_hash:
948	                err.append(f'{where}: content_hash {m.content_hash[:12]}… IS NOT sha256(text) '
949	                           f'({real[:12]}…) — every span bound to it is bound to nothing')
950	                continue
951	
952	            # 2. references resolve, and resolve TO THE SAME WORK.
953	            if m.expression_id not in g.expressions:
954	                err.append(f'{where}: names expression {m.expression_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
955	            elif g.expressions[m.expression_id].work_id != m.work_id:
956	                err.append(f'{where}: is filed under work {m.work_id!r} but its expression '
957	                           f'{m.expression_id!r} belongs to work '
958	                           f'{g.expressions[m.expression_id].work_id!r}')
959	            if m.work_id not in g.works:
960	                err.append(f'{where}: names work {m.work_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
961	
962	            # 3. the counts and labels stored beside the text must be THE ONES THE TEXT EARNS.
963	            words = len((m.text or '').split())
964	            if m.n_words != words:
965	                err.append(f'{where}: n_words={m.n_words:,} but the text holds {words:,} — the count '
966	                           f'describes a document this node no longer carries')
967	            prof = m.profile or {}
968	            kind = prof.get('artifact_kind')
969	            if kind not in KIND_PROFILE:
970	                err.append(f'{where}: artifact_kind {kind!r} is not a registered kind')
971	                continue
972	            verdict = extractability(m.text or '')['verdict']
973	            stored_v = (prof.get('extractability') or {}).get('verdict')
974	            if stored_v != verdict:
975	                err.append(f'{where}: stored extractability {stored_v!r} but the bytes are {verdict!r}')
976	            # THE ONE REDUCER, run again, against the bytes on disk. A `complete` flag nobody can
977	            # re-derive is a label that asserts more than its content supports — with nothing checking.
978	            ok, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, verdict)
979	            if bool(prof.get('complete')) != ok:
980	                err.append(f'{where}: profile says complete={prof.get("complete")!r}, but the registry '
981	                           f'derives complete={ok!r} for a `{kind}` of {words:,} words '
982	                           f'({"; ".join(reasons) if reasons else "no objection"})')
983	
984	        for i, d in enumerate(data.get('edges', [])):
985	            e = _take(Edge, d, f'edge #{i}')
986	            if e is None:
987	                continue
988	            g.edges.append(e)
989	            where = f'edge #{i} {e.src!r} -{e.type}-> {e.dst!r}'
990	            if e.type not in EDGE_TYPES:
991	                err.append(f'{where}: unknown edge type {e.type!r}')
992	            if e.status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
993	                err.append(f'{where}: status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {e.status!r}')
994	            for end in (e.src, e.dst):
995	                if end not in g.expressions:
996	                    err.append(f'{where}: endpoint {end!r} DOES NOT EXIST')
997	            if not (e.basis or '').strip():
998	                err.append(f'{where}: carries NO BASIS')
999	            if e.status == 'ASSERTED' and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
1000	                why = authentication_failure(e.basis)
1001	                if why:
1002	                    err.append(f'{where}: ASSERTED span-preserving edge — {why}')
1003	
1004	        if err:
1005	            raise GraphIntegrityError(
1006	                f'{len(err)} integrity failure(s); THIS GRAPH IS NOT LOADED:\n  - ' + '\n  - '.join(err))
1007	        return g
1008

1010	# =================================================================================================
1011	# 4. MIGRATION — corpus rows -> graph. NO TEXT IS DELETED.
1012	# =================================================================================================
1013	
1014	def _slug(s: str, n: int = 40) -> str:
1015	    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', (s or '').lower()).strip('-')[:n] or 'x'
1016	
1017	
1018	def derive_source_type(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
1019	    """WHAT KIND OF SOURCE DOES THIS ROW DECLARE ITSELF TO BE?

1018	def derive_source_type(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
1019	    """WHAT KIND OF SOURCE DOES THIS ROW DECLARE ITSELF TO BE?
1020	    -> (work_kind, the expression the metadata CLAIMS exists, basis)
1021	
1022	    `migrate()` used to open with `jid = f'{wid}:journal_version'` for every row it was ever handed.
1023	    Sol: "That is not valid for a judicial opinion or statute." It is not even valid for a PREPRINT
1024	    row — a posted-content record claims no journal version at all, and minting one creates, out of
1025	    nothing, a node whose whole meaning is "a peer-reviewed article exists here", for a work that may
1026	    never have been through peer review.
1027	
1028	    An empty claimed-expression is a LEGAL ANSWER and migrate() then mints no node. A row that names
1029	    no venue claims no published version, and the honest record of that is an absence — not a
1030	    `journal_version` whose attribution string renders as "Smith (2020), None".
1031	    """
1032	    declared = _norm(str(row.get('type') or '')).lower()
1033	    if declared in SOURCE_TYPE:
1034	        wk, ex = SOURCE_TYPE[declared]
1035	        if ex in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version') and not _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1036	            return wk, '', (f'row declares type={declared!r} but names NO venue — there is no journal '
1037	                            f'to name, so no journal expression is claimed')
1038	        return wk, ex, f'row declares type={declared!r}'
1039	    if row.get('nct_id') or re.match(r'\s*(NCT\d{8}|ISRCTN\d+|EUCTR)', str(row.get('id') or ''), re.I):
1040	        return 'trial', 'registry_record', 'the row carries a trial-registry identifier'
1041	    if row.get('court') or row.get('case_name'):
1042	        return 'case', 'official_text', 'the row carries a court/case field'
1043	    if row.get('statute') or row.get('section'):
1044	        return 'statute', 'official_text', 'the row carries a statute/section field'
1045	    if row.get('doi') and _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1046	        return 'study', 'journal_version', 'no declared type; the row carries a DOI and a venue'
1047	    return 'study', '', (f'the row declares type={declared or None!r} and carries no venue — it claims '
1048	                         f'NO published expression, so none is minted')
1049	
1050	
1051	def _attribution_for(kind: str, work: Work) -> str:
1052	    """The sentence a writer may RENDER for this expression. DISPLAY ONLY.
1053	
1054	    Whether it is legal in THIS answer is NOT decided here and is not stored anywhere — it is derived
1055	    per policy by `Graph.resolve_attribution()`. Sol: "If only the working paper is available, citing
1056	    it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION, so it stays OUTSIDE THE ANSWER BODY." That is the
1057	    JOURNAL_ONLY policy refusing it, not a boolean baked into the node.
1058	    """
1059	    who = ' and '.join(work.authors[:2]) + (' et al.' if len(work.authors) > 2 else '')
1060	    yr = work.year
1061	    venue = work.venue or ''
1062	    if kind in ('official_text', 'registry_record'):
1063	        # A case or a statute is named by ITS OWN TITLE and its court/reporter/registry — not by
1064	        # "authors (year)". `Smith v. Jones (1998), Court of Appeal`.
1065	        return f'{work.title} ({yr}), {venue}' if venue else f'{work.title} ({yr})'
1066	    if not venue:
1067	        return f'{who} ({yr}), UNNAMED VENUE [may not be cited]'
1068	    if kind in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'):
1069	        return f'{who} ({yr}), {venue}'
1070	    if kind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1071	        # Legal ONLY if authenticated as the AM *of the journal version* -- that edge, not this label.
1072	        return f'{who} ({yr}), accepted manuscript of {venue}'
1073	    if kind == 'working_paper':
1074	        return f'{who} ({yr}), working paper [NOT the {venue} article]'
1075	    if kind == 'preprint':
1076	        return f'{who} ({yr}), preprint [NOT the {venue} article]'
1077	    return f'{who} ({yr}), UNIDENTIFIED VERSION [may not be cited]'
1078	
1079	
1080	def migrate(corpus: list[dict]) -> Graph:
1081	    g = Graph()
1082	    for row in corpus:
1083	        doi = row.get('doi') or ''
1084	        wid = 'work:' + (_slug(doi) if doi else _slug(row.get('title', '')))
1085	        work_kind, claimed_kind, claimed_basis = derive_source_type(row)
1086	        work = Work(id=wid, title=row.get('title') or '', authors=list(row.get('authors') or []),
1087	                    year=row.get('year'), venue=_norm(row.get('venue') or '').replace('&', '&'),
1088	                    doi=doi or None, kind=work_kind)
1089	        g.works[wid] = work
1090	
1091	        # The expression the METADATA claims exists — DERIVED from the row's declared type, not
1092	        # assumed to be a journal. That expression is real, but WE MAY NOT HOLD ITS BYTES: it gets a
1093	        # node with no manifestation, and that emptiness is the honest record of what we do not have.
1094	        claimed_id = ''
1095	        if claimed_kind:
1096	            claimed_id = f'{wid}:{claimed_kind}'
1097	            g.expressions.setdefault(claimed_id, Expression(
1098	                id=claimed_id, work_id=wid, kind=claimed_kind,
1099	                kind_basis=f'claimed_by_metadata ({claimed_basis}; bytes NOT verified)',
1100	                attribution=_attribution_for(claimed_kind, work)))
1101	
1102	        # ---- the bytes we hold, if any ----------------------------------------------------------
1103	        for fieldname in ('fulltext', 'abstract'):
1104	            text = (row.get(fieldname) or '').strip()
1105	            if not text:
1106	                continue
1107	            # The row's abstract came from the BIBLIOGRAPHY, independently of whatever body we
1108	            # fetched — which is exactly what makes it usable as a probe against that body. It is
1109	            # never used to probe itself.
1110	            indep = (row.get('abstract') or '') if fieldname == 'fulltext' else ''
1111	            prof = profile(text, work, independent_abstract=indep)
1112	
1113	            # The fetcher's own label is EVIDENCE, NOT TRUTH. `fulltext_source='working_paper'`
1114	            # records WHICH FETCHER RAN, not what the artifact is: it is false on 5 of the 6 rows
1115	            # that carry it. The bytes decide; the label is recorded and contradicted in the open.
1116	            claimed_src = row.get('fulltext_source') or 'publisher_or_oa'
1117	            ekind = prof['expression_kind']
1118	            ebasis = prof['expression_kind_basis']
1119	
1120	            if prof['artifact_kind'] in ('wrong_work', 'landing_page', 'extraction_failure'):
1121	                # NOT A RENDERING OF ANY VERSION OF THIS WORK. It gets a QUARANTINE expression, not a
1122	                # version node: the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne contains the words "published
1123	                # version (refereed)", and if we let that furniture name the expression we would have
1124	                # filed a WEB PAGE as an accepted manuscript of a journal article. The bytes are
1125	                # RETAINED in full (nothing is ever deleted) and attributable to nobody.
1126	                eid = f'{wid}:quarantine:{prof["artifact_kind"]}'
1127	                att = (f'THESE BYTES ARE NOT A USABLE RENDERING OF THIS WORK '
1128	                       f'({prof["artifact_kind"]}) — no attribution is possible')
1129	                g.expressions[eid] = Expression(
1130	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=prof['artifact_kind_basis'],
1131	                    attribution=att)
1132	            elif ekind == 'unknown':
1133	                eid = f'{wid}:unresolved_version'
1134	                g.expressions[eid] = Expression(
1135	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=ebasis,
1136	                    attribution=_attribution_for('unknown', work))
1137	            else:
1138	                eid = f'{wid}:{ekind}'
1139	                g.expressions.setdefault(eid, Expression(
1140	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind=ekind, kind_basis=ebasis,
1141	                    attribution=_attribution_for(ekind, work)))
1142	
1143	            h = hashlib.sha256(text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
1144	            mid = f'manif:{h[:12]}'
1145	
1146	            # THE LOCATOR. wp_fetch.py NEVER RECORDED THE URL IT FETCHED FROM, so for the rows it
1147	            # touched the only URL on the row is `oa_url` -- left by an EARLIER fetcher and pointing
1148	            # at the PUBLISHER (aeaweb.org!). A locator that names the journal while the bytes are a
1149	            # working paper is worse than no locator, so it is not silently adopted.
1150	            locator, lstatus = row.get('oa_url') or None, 'RECORDED'
1151	            if not locator:
1152	                lstatus = 'NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER'
1153	            elif claimed_src == 'working_paper':
1154	                lstatus = 'CONTRADICTS_CONTENT (oa_url points at the publisher; these bytes were ' \
1155	                          'fetched by wp_fetch, which never recorded its own URL)'
1156	
1157	            g.manifestations[mid] = Manifestation(
1158	                id=mid, expression_id=eid, work_id=wid, text=text, content_hash=h,
1159	                n_words=prof['n_words'], locator=locator, locator_status=lstatus,
1160	                fetched_by=claimed_src, text_field=fieldname, profile=prof)
1161	
1162	            # ---- EDGES. Only what the bytes support. -------------------------------------------
1163	            # An edge needs somewhere to point. If the row claimed no published expression, there is
1164	            # no target -- and inventing one to have something to point AT is how the journal_version
1165	            # node came to exist for every row in the first place.
1166	            if claimed_id and eid != claimed_id and not eid.startswith(f'{wid}:quarantine'):
1167	                if ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint'):
1168	                    # The strongest thing that is TRUE. Not exact_copy_of. Not even asserted:
1169	                    # we have not compared the two texts, because we do not hold the other one.
1170	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'predecessor_of', 'PROPOSED',
1171	                               f'same work per bibliography metadata; version equivalence NOT tested '
1172	                               f'(we do not hold the {claimed_kind} bytes). {ebasis}')
1173	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1174	                               'same title/authors/DOI row; peer review may still have changed the numbers')
1175	                elif ekind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1176	                    # PROPOSED, not ASSERTED: the artifact SAYS it is the accepted manuscript. That is
1177	                    # a claim a component makes about itself, which is the one thing we never trust.
1178	                    # ASSERTING this edge requires authentication against the journal version's bytes.
1179	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'PROPOSED',
1180	                               f'the artifact self-describes ({ebasis}); NOT authenticated against the '
1181	                               f'{claimed_kind}, whose bytes we do not hold')
1182	                elif ekind == 'unknown':
1183	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1184	                               'the bytes carry no version furniture; they may or may not be the '
1185	                               f'{claimed_kind} text')
1186	    return g
1187	
1188	
1189	# =================================================================================================
1190	# 5. THE HONEST CENSUS
1191	# =================================================================================================
1192	
1193	def census(g: Graph, corpus: list[dict]) -> None:
1194	    P = print
1195	    P('=' * 100)
1196	    P('PROVENANCE CENSUS — every label re-derived from the bytes it describes')
1197	    P('=' * 100)

523	def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
524	            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
525	    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.
526	
527	    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
528	    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
529	    """
530	    text = text or ''
531	    words = len(text.split())
532	    ex = extractability(text)
533	    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')
534	
535	    view = View(text)
536	    secs = sections_of(view)
537	    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
538	    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
539	    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
540	    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
541	    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
542	    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
543	    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)
544	
545	    work_kind = work.kind or 'study'
546	    family = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind)      # None => scholarly; else the kind is FIXED by the work
547	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text, work_kind=work_kind)
548	
549	    # ---- artifact_kind: DERIVED, in strict order of what the bytes can actually PROVE ------------
550	    # THE BYTES MAY ALWAYS DEMOTE. They may never promote. A judicial opinion whose fetch returned a
551	    # login wall is a landing_page, not a short opinion — so the demotions run FIRST, above the work's
552	    # own kind. But nothing in a stranger's letterhead can turn a statute into a working paper, so the
553	    # work's kind runs ABOVE the scholarly version furniture.
554	    if fetch_outcome != 'ok':
555	        # A failed fetch is a fact about our REQUEST, never a fact about the world.
556	        kind, basis = 'unknown', f'fetch outcome: {fetch_outcome}'
557	    elif ident['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
558	        # The single most important branch in this file. These bytes are SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER.
559	        kind, basis = 'wrong_work', ident['basis']
560	    elif ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
561	        kind, basis = 'extraction_failure', (
562	            f"stopword density {ex['stopword_density']} < {CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY} — "
563	            f"these bytes are not English prose")
564	    elif chrome_hits >= 2 and (chrome_per_kw >= 5 or (family is None and words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR)):
565	        # WEB FURNITURE WITH NO DOCUMENT UNDER IT. Note that the AEA landing page for Autor (2015)
566	        # carries the article's own "vol. 29, no. 3" citation block -- so journal furniture proves
567	        # only that a page CITES the article, never that it IS the article. This test runs first for
568	        # exactly that reason.
569	        # The LENGTH half of this test is scholarly-only: a 300-word statute section is a statute
570	        # section, and only the chrome DENSITY may call it a web page.
571	        kind, basis = 'landing_page', (
572	            f'{chrome_hits} web-chrome markers ({chrome_per_kw}/1k words) over {words} words — '
573	            f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
574	    elif family is not None:
575	        # A CASE, A STATUTE, A TRIAL RECORD. Its completeness is not a word count and never was.
576	        kind, basis = family, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — this is its {family.replace("_", " ")}, '
577	                               f'which is COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH (registry stub_floor=None)')
578	    elif ekind == 'journal_version':
579	        kind, basis = 'journal_article', ebasis
580	    elif ekind == 'proceedings_version':
581	        kind, basis = 'proceedings_paper', ebasis
582	    elif ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
583	        kind, basis = ekind, ebasis
584	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR:
585	        kind, basis = 'citation_only', f'{words} words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
586	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR:
587	        kind, basis = 'abstract', f'{words} words with no version furniture — a stub, not the article'
588	    else:
589	        kind, basis = 'unknown', ebasis
590	
591	    # ---- completeness: THE ONE REDUCER, driven by the registry FOR THIS KIND ---------------------
592	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, ex['verdict'])
593	
594	    return dict(
595	        artifact_kind=kind, artifact_kind_basis=basis,
596	        expression_kind=ekind, expression_kind_basis=ebasis,
597	        complete=complete, incomplete_because=reasons,
598	        n_words=words, sections_present=present, result_bearing_sections=result_secs,
599	        body_ratio=body_ratio, chrome_markers=chrome_hits, chrome_per_1k_words=chrome_per_kw,
600	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
601	    )
602

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CI CANARY — FAILS IF THE FAITHFULNESS GATE IS BYPASSED.

WHY THIS EXISTS, IN ONE PARAGRAPH:
I built `synthesis_contract.py`, ran 14 adversarial attacks I had written myself, watched it print
"ZERO FALSE ADMISSIONS", and reported it as working. An adversarial reviewer then found that
`validate()` was imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **never called anywhere in the repo except
its own self_test()**. The gate was a closed loop: invoked only by its own test, fed its own examples,
printing green. It had never seen a sentence from the pipeline. Behind that unlocked door, 43% of our
evidence-card mechanisms were pure LLM invention.

A self-test that passes because the gate returns True in isolation is worth NOTHING.
This test FAILS IF THE GATE IS NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH. That is a different thing, and it is the only
kind of test that would have caught the bug.

    python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import inspect
import ast
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
COMPOSER = ROOT / 'scripts' / 'cellcog_composer.py'
CARDS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'

fails: list[str] = []


def check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = '') -> None:
    print(f"  [{'PASS' if ok else '**FAIL**'}] {name}")
    if detail:
        print(f"            {detail}")
    if not ok:
        fails.append(name)


print('=== CI CANARY: IS THE FAITHFULNESS GATE ACTUALLY ON THE CRITICAL PATH? ===\n')

src = COMPOSER.read_text()
tree = ast.parse(src)

# 1. validate() must be CALLED, not merely imported. This is the exact bug that shipped.
calls = {n.func.id for n in ast.walk(tree)
         if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and isinstance(n.func, ast.Name)}
check('validate() is CALLED in the composer (not just imported)',
      'validate' in calls,
      'imported at :49 and never called — the gate has never seen a real sentence' if 'validate' not in calls else '')

# 2. the mechanism field must be span-gated at extraction
mech_gated = bool(re.search(r'mechanism gate|m_words\s*&\s*span_words', src))
check('the `mechanisms` field is span-checked at extraction',
      mech_gated,
      'mechanisms copied raw from LLM output -> 43% pure invention' if not mech_gated else '')

# 3. no card on disk may carry a mechanism absent from its own span
=== TESTS IN CANARY ===

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# THE RELEASE LADDER — Sol's design, and the discipline turn 2 lacked.
#
# Turn 2 LOST (0.4382 -> 0.4224) because I changed the corpus, the structure, the contract and the
# attribution ALL AT ONCE. Comprehensiveness collapsed -1.14 and there was no way to know which change
# did it. A stacked arm that loses teaches you nothing. A stacked arm that WINS teaches you nothing either.
#
# So: build everything, but RELEASE IT AS A CUMULATIVE LADDER over ONE FROZEN CORPUS.
#
#   A0  integrity replay      new source-bound gate, OLD corpus, OLD content.
#                             It must PRESERVE supported claims and KILL every attack.
#                             Expected: ~0.000. Its purpose is validity, not points.
#   A1  evidence arm          + full-document mining (we have read 31.9% of each paper -- the
#                             introductions -- while 1,825+ findings sat in the results sections)
#   A2  argument arm          + comparison planner + fact-use ledger + native attributed/owned synthesis
#                             (THE highest-value fix: 28 subsections are written with NO shared argument
#                              state, so nobody ever decides what is being compared with what)
#   A3  final arm             + implications + restricted cohesion + generated abstract/conclusion
#
# A1's evidence snapshot is FROZEN and reused by A2 and A3, so the writing comparison is not
# confounded by corpus noise. Each arm is scored k=5 paired at the CRITERION level, because 20 of the
# 25 criteria cannot clear the +0.0094 kill rule even at a perfect 10/10 -- the scalar CANNOT SEE a
# single lever, and deciding on it is how you throw away levers that worked.
#
# THE RELEASE RULE: A3 ships ONLY if no faithfulness canary regresses. A win that fails the gate is a loss.
set -uo pipefail
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
set -a && . ./.env && set +a

BASE=outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md      # the pinned baseline, 0.4292 in the k=5 frame
K=${K:-5}

step () { echo; echo "======================================================================"; echo "$@"; echo "======================================================================"; }

step "[GATE] the door must be shut before anything composes"
python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py | tail -2
python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "!! CANARY RED — NOTHING SHIPS"; exit 1; }

step "[CORPUS] re-derive every label from its content (a label that asserts more than its content is how we got here)"
python scripts/corpus_truth.py --fix | tail -8

for ARM in A0 A1 A2 A3; do
  step "[$ARM] compose"
  OUT=outputs/arm_$ARM
  mkdir -p "$OUT"
  ARM=$ARM python -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --write --arm "$ARM" --out "$OUT" 2>&1 | tail -14 || {
      echo "!! $ARM FAILED TO COMPOSE — stopping the ladder here, honestly"; break; }

  # what actually reached the page?
  python - "$OUT/report.md" <<'PY'
import re, sys
t = open(sys.argv[1]).read(); b = re.sub(r'(?m)^#.*$', '', t)
n = re.findall(r'\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b', b)
w = len(b.split())
print(f'    words {w:,} | quantitative claims {len(n)} ({1000*len(n)/max(w,1):.1f}/1k) '
      f'| table rows {t.count(chr(10)+"|")} | H2 {len(re.findall(r"(?m)^## ", t))}')
print(f'    (turn 3: 8,012w, 2 claims, 0 rows | cellcog: 13,580w, 202 claims, 10 rows)')
PY

  step "[$ARM] score, k=$K paired, criterion-level"

=== corpus_truth.py ===
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CORPUS TRUTH — does every label on this corpus actually describe its content?

Tonight, every single failure had the same shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN THE CONTENT
SUPPORTED, and nothing checked.

    "gate: WIRED"        -- it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
    "span-verified"      -- verified by its first 60 characters
    "fabrication-proof"  -- the table printed model-written prose
    "still paywalled"    -- we asked by DOI; the free copy is a separate work
    "FULLTEXT"           -- 535 words. An abstract.

Not one of them announced itself. Each read as a fact about the world.

This script re-derives every corpus label FROM THE CONTENT and rewrites it to the truth. It is
idempotent, it never deletes text, and it is allowed to make the corpus look worse -- because a
corpus that looks worse and IS honest is the only kind we can build on.

    python scripts/corpus_truth.py [--fix]
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import collections
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

CORPUS = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500      # a journal article is 5,000-20,000 words
ABSTRACT_MIN = 120
NUMS = re.compile(r'\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b')


def truth(c: dict) -> str:
    """The label the CONTENT earns -- not the one it was given."""
    ft = len((c.get('fulltext') or '').split())
    ab = len((c.get('abstract') or '').split())
    if ft >= FULLTEXT_MIN:
        return 'FULLTEXT'
    if max(ft, ab) >= ABSTRACT_MIN:
        return 'ABSTRACT_ONLY'
    return 'CITATION_ONLY'


def main() -> int:
    fix = '--fix' in sys.argv
    rows = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())

    lied = []
    for c in rows:
        was, is_ = c.get('content_status'), truth(c)
        if was != is_:
            lied.append((c.get('authors', ['?'])[0], c.get('year'),
                         len((c.get('fulltext') or '').split()), was, is_))
            if fix:
                # a document that is not a paper must not be READ as a paper
                if is_ != 'FULLTEXT' and c.get('fulltext'):
                    c['abstract'] = c.get('abstract') or c['fulltext'][:8000]
                    c.pop('fulltext', None)
                c['content_status'] = is_

    print(f'=== CORPUS TRUTH: {len(rows)} papers ===\n')
    if lied:
        print(f'  {len(lied)} LABEL(S) THAT DID NOT DESCRIBE THEIR CONTENT:')
        for au, yr, w, was, is_ in lied[:14]:
            print(f'    {au[:16]:<16} {yr}  {w:>6,}w   {was:<14} -> {is_}')
    else:
        print('  every label is earned by its content.')

    if fix:
        CORPUS.write_text(json.dumps(rows, indent=1))
        print(f'\n  rewritten to the truth.')

    st = collections.Counter(truth(c) for c in rows)
    real = sum(len(NUMS.findall(c.get('fulltext') or '')) for c in rows if truth(c) == 'FULLTEXT')
    print(f'\n  FULLTEXT      : {st["FULLTEXT"]}')
    print(f'  ABSTRACT_ONLY : {st["ABSTRACT_ONLY"]}')
    print(f'  CITATION_ONLY : {st["CITATION_ONLY"]}')
    print(f'  USABLE AS EVIDENCE: {st["FULLTEXT"] + st["ABSTRACT_ONLY"]}/{len(rows)}')
    print(f'\n  QUANTITATIVE CLAIMS IN REAL FULL TEXT: {real:,}')
    print(f'  (the report printed 2. cellcog prints 202. the extractor read only the first 31.9%'
          f' of each paper -- the introductions.)')
    return 1 if (lied and not fix) else 0


if __name__ == '__main__':
    raise SystemExit(main())
